what_it_does: Implements a multi-scale embedding pyramid over an existing vector space: it hierarchically clusters unit vectors into coarser theme rungs, persists the cluster centroids as queryable vectors in scale-specific spaces, and exposes load, rung-listing, and rung-query functions.
observations: The module opens with a long design-rationale docstring explaining the shift from lineage-based to meaning-based coarsening and the choice of Ward linkage.
It implements agglomerative clustering from scratch in pure Python, with Ward, complete, and average linkage supported via a Lance-Williams update.
Squared Euclidean distance on L2-normalized vectors is used as a cosine-equivalent distance (d² = 2(1 - cosine)).
A single dendrogram is cut at multiple K values, so every coarser partition is a strict coarsening of every finer partition.
Cluster centroids are persisted as ordinary vectors in the existing vector substrate under space names like scale:<space>:k<K>.
Persistence is incremental: a centroid is rewritten only when a hash of its members' source/content_hash pairs changes.
Empty or too-thin spaces raise rather than returning silent empty pyramids.
For spaces above 4000 units, the ward path delegates to a MiniBatchKMeans-plus-Ward hybrid that preserves nesting by unioning fine clusters.
The embedder layer is threaded explicitly through build and query calls to avoid querying a different layer than the one used to build the pyramid.
Cluster labels and ordering are deterministic because clusters are sorted by their smallest member index.
standouts: Hand-rolled agglomerative Ward clustering with no numpy/scipy dependency for small spaces.
A kmeans-plus-Ward hybrid that keeps the exact nesting invariant for large spaces.
Cluster centroids stored as first-class queryable vectors in the same vector store as units.
Incremental content-hash skip logic for cluster centroid persistence.
conventions: Extensive argumentative docstrings that cite evidence and design reversals.
Fail-loud behavior for empty, thin, or inconsistent states.
Explicit threading of the embedder layer parameter (emb) through build and query paths.
Incremental persistence keyed by content hash.
Reuse of existing store and vector_index abstractions rather than a parallel index.
Type hints on public function signatures.
concerns: The pure-Python agglomerate is O(n³) time and O(n²) memory, which may be slow or heavy near the 4000-unit cutoff.
_WARD_MAX_UNITS is a hardcoded constant with no adaptive memory or timing check.
build_scale_pyramid_kmeans imports numpy and sklearn at runtime; absence of either raises ImportError with no fallback.
_member_hash performs a store lookup per member per cluster, which could be expensive for large spaces.
active.remove calls inside the agglomeration loop are O(n), compounding the cubic cost.
There is no upfront dimension validation for input vectors; mismatches surface only during distance computation.
If a caller passes linkage other than ward on a large space, the function does not delegate and may attempt the expensive pure-Python path.
notes: A dense, opinionated clustering module that adds a zoom axis to the embedding instrument by turning unit vectors into nested theme centroids stored in the same vector substrate.
questions: What are the exact contracts of store.index_corpus, store.put_vector, store.save_scale_pyramid, store.load_scale_pyramid, and store.space_address?
How does the emb layer affect vector keying and the behavior of query_index?
What vector dimension and numeric type are expected in the input list-of-floats vectors?
How does the system handle re-embedding that changes a unit vector but not its content_hash?
Why is _WARD_MAX_UNITS fixed at 4000 rather than derived from available memory or measured cost?
What happens if MiniBatchKMeans produces empty clusters for the finest rung?
How are caller-supplied rungs validated against the floor and per-cluster member-count caps?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds hierarchical scale rungs, persists centroids into the existing vector store, and exposes rung-based queries, with a scalable kmeans path for large spaces.
apparent_themes: Meaning-based coarsening rather than provenance-based hierarchy.
Reuse of the existing vector substrate and query index.
Scalability through an exact/approximate hybrid clustering path.
intention_signals: The file is trying to add a zoom axis to an embedding instrument so the same semantic renderer and query pipeline can resolve either individual units or coarse theme clusters depending on the chosen scale rung, without re-embedding or building a separate vector system.
novelty: A dependency-free, hand-rolled agglomerative Ward implementation that operates on L2-normalized vectors to approximate cosine Ward; a kmeans-plus-Ward hybrid that preserves exact nesting for large spaces; and treating cluster centroids as first-class queryable vectors stored in the same substrate as units.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, incremental multi-resolution clustering layer that reuses the existing vector store and query index to let the instrument resolve queries at coarse theme rungs or fine unit rungs without re-embedding.
summary_for_embedding: A pure-Python hierarchical clustering and coarse-centroid persistence module that builds multi-resolution scale rungs over an existing embedding space, storing cluster centroids as queryable vectors and exposing rung-based resolution.
intention_for_embedding: To add a deterministic, reusable zoom axis to the embedding instrument so queries can resolve themes at coarse scales or units at fine scales without re-embedding.

symbols.does: L2-normalizes a vector represented as a list of floats.
symbols.description: Computes the Euclidean norm and divides each component; it guards against a zero norm by returning the original vector unchanged, enabling cosine-equivalent Ward clustering on normalized vectors.

symbols.does: Computes the cosine similarity of two already-normalized vectors.
symbols.description: Returns the dot product after verifying the dimensions match; the dimension check is a fail-loud guard against silently truncated comparisons.

symbols.does: Computes the L2-normalized mean of selected normalized vectors.
symbols.description: Averages the member vectors by index and re-normalizes the result so the centroid lives on the same unit sphere as the units, making cosine comparisons meaningful.

symbols.does: Builds a single agglomerative dendrogram and records partitions at every cluster count.
symbols.description: Implements Ward, complete, or average linkage from scratch in pure Python using squared Euclidean distances on L2-normalized vectors; it returns normalized vectors and a cuts dictionary where every coarser partition is a strict coarsening of every finer one.

symbols.does: Looks up the stored squared distance between two cluster ids.
symbols.description: A small helper that indexes the upper-triangle distance dictionary in either order so the agglomeration loop can treat distances symmetrically.
A nested closure that maps every original point to its current cluster id and stores the partition under the current number of active clusters.

symbols.does: Returns the partition whose cluster count is closest to the requested k.
symbols.description: If an exact k snapshot exists it returns it; otherwise it chooses the nearest available count, breaking ties toward the finer (larger k) partition.

symbols.does: Inverts a point-label partition into a label-to-members mapping.
symbols.description: Collects point indices per cluster label in ascending index order, producing stable and reproducible cluster groupings.

symbols.does: Proposes a sparse dyadic resolution ladder for a space of n units.
symbols.description: Computes a coarse and fine rung near a power of two around sqrt(n), capped so clusters average at least three members; it returns descending cluster counts or an empty list if the space is too thin to coarsen.

symbols.does: Computes an incremental content hash for a cluster centroid.
symbols.description: Sorts member source addresses and concatenates their stored content hashes, then hashes the result with the same blake2b helper used for unit vectors, so centroids are re-persisted only when membership or member embeddings change.

symbols.does: Builds and persists a multi-scale pyramid over the unit vectors of a space.
symbols.description: Chooses rungs, runs agglomerative clustering, persists each cluster centroid as a queryable vector in a scale-specific space, records membership, exemplar, and parent links, and saves the pyramid structure; it delegates to a kmeans-hybrid path for spaces larger than _WARD_MAX_UNITS.

symbols.does: Builds a scalable pyramid for large spaces using MiniBatchKMeans plus Ward on centroids.
symbols.description: Clusters all units into the finest rung with sklearn, then hierarchically agglomerates the fine centroids for coarser rungs, preserving exact nesting by unioning fine clusters; it uses numpy for in-place normalization and memory efficiency.

symbols.does: Computes the normalized mean of rows selected from the in-memory numpy array.
symbols.description: A helper inside the kmeans path that averages the unit-normalized member vectors and re-normalizes, mirroring the pure-Python centroid logic with numpy operations.

symbols.does: Persists all clusters for one rung and records their metadata.
symbols.description: A nested helper that computes exemplars, checks the incremental member hash against stored vectors, writes or skips centroid vectors, and appends cluster records with optional finer-child links.

symbols.does: Loads the persisted pyramid structure for a space and embedder layer.
symbols.description: A thin wrapper around store.load_scale_pyramid that returns None if no pyramid exists, without fabricating an empty one.

symbols.does: Returns the coarse cluster points for a given rung.
symbols.description: Reads the saved pyramid and the corresponding centroid vectors from the vector substrate, returning source, centroid, size, exemplar, members, and child links for each cluster.

symbols.does: Resolves a query vector at a chosen scale rung.
symbols.description: Queries the existing vector index over either the original unit space or the coarse scale space, threading the embedder layer explicitly so the query uses the same layer the pyramid was built on.
